Case reference: validation_original_20260703_215912
Generated: 03 July 2026, 23:23
This report presents investigative leads based on transaction pattern analysis. It does not establish guilt — all findings should be independently verified before action.

Case Narrative

In brief, for the investigating officer:

  • A total of Rs 280,463,403.50 moved across the accounts in this case, with Rs 51,066,490.81 coming from outside the suspect network.
  • SNEHA MALHOTRA sent out Rs 48,402,511.80, making it the largest source of funds.
  • There were 15 intermediary accounts that received and quickly passed on funds.
  • Sarita Deepa spread money to 825 different accounts.
  • PIONEER TRADING collected money from 543 different accounts.
  • Funds accumulated in account 898151376.
  • Rs 202,820,913.30 of incoming funds was traced to onward transfers or withdrawals, while Rs 9,917,778.00 could not be fully traced.
  • The account to investigate first is Sarita Deepa, which shows the strongest combination of suspicious behaviors in this case.

3 · Analysis Summary

The engine ran 160 account(s) through the full deterministic detector suite and built a money-flow graph of 4,044 nodes and 32,415 transaction edges.

4 · Findings by Account

Showing the 12 highest-priority accounts in full detail. All 115 flagged accounts are ranked in the Final Summary table.

Sarita Deepa strong
acct_025 · Unknown Bank · Account Number 17496072039317 · Score 7,505
Rank #1

This account is suspicious due to its involvement in multiple unusual financial activities, including shared UPI identifiers, low-value account testing, and round-trip transactions. The account's behavior suggests potential coordinated activity or money laundering. These activities warrant further investigation.

  • Shared Upi Identifiers strong — UPI identifier '0676053000004298@sibl000' is shared across 2 accounts: acct_025 and acct_106, indicating possible shared ownership or coordinated activity. (+492 more of this type)
  • Low Value Account Testing strong — Accounts 9207484954@ybl and acct_025 exchanged 10 low-value transactions, consistent with account-testing behavior, followed by larger transfers. (+50 more of this type)
  • Round Trip Detection strong — Money moved from acct_025 to acct_106 and returned within 0 days at comparable amounts, indicating potential round-trip transaction activity. (+1 more of this type)
  • Duplicate Verification weak — Transactions acct_025_019134 and acct_025_019138 have identical amounts, dates, and narration, suggesting duplicate transactions. (+132 more of this type)
  • Cross Statement Links weak — A debit of 100.00 from acct_025 matches a credit of 100.00 to acct_106 on 2019-12-24, indicating a linked transaction. (+61 more of this type)
  • Pass Through Routing Account weak — Account acct_025 processed 36509229.62 with a 100.0% throughput ratio, suggesting pass-through activity. (+1 more of this type)
  • Money Flow Graph Construction structural — A money-flow graph with 4044 nodes and 32415 edges was constructed, indicating complex transaction activity.
  • Hub Ranking weak — Account acct_025 acts as a routing hub with 4 inbound and 4 outbound connections and a total flow volume of 22522077.60.
  • Round Value Debit Patterns weak — Account acct_025 made 11 suspicious round-value debits totalling 351000.00, with repeated amounts: ₹20,000 x4.

This account is suspicious due to unusual money behavior, including round trips and large transfers. The account exhibits patterns of money laundering, such as round-value debits and hub-like activity. These behaviors warrant further investigation.

  • Round Trip Detection strong — Money moved from acct_076 to acct_077 and back within 0 days, indicating a potential round trip. (+33 more of this type)
  • Money Trail Tracing strong — A credit of 3800000.00 was traced into 2 subsequent debits from acct_077_077710 on 2024-10-05. (+16 more of this type)
  • Circular Flow Multi Hop Cycle Detection strong — A 4-account cycle was detected: acct_005 → acct_018 → acct_076 → acct_077 → acct_005, suggesting circular flow. (+2 more of this type)
  • First Contact Large Transfer strong — A large transfer of 590000.00 occurred between acct_076 and acct_077 on 2024-05-08 with no prior relationship. (+2 more of this type)
  • Money Flow Graph Construction structural — A money-flow graph with 4044 nodes and 32415 edges was constructed, indicating complex transaction activity.
  • Hub Ranking weak — Acct_077 acts as a routing hub with 1 inbound and 4 outbound connections, and a total flow volume of 92960000.00.
  • Round Value Debit Patterns weak — Acct_077 made 8 suspicious round-value debits totalling 12723000.00, which may indicate structured transactions.

SUMIT YADAV strong
acct_129 · State Bank of India · Account Number 38347344323 · Score 3,472
Rank #12

This account is suspicious due to its involvement in a 3-account cycle and shared UPI identifiers across 14 accounts, indicating potential coordinated activity or shared ownership. The account also exhibits systematic transaction reversal activity and round-value debit patterns. These behaviors warrant further investigation.

  • Shared Upi Identifiers strong — UPI identifier 'aarav@ybl' appears across 14 different accounts, indicating potential shared ownership or coordinated activity. (+14 more of this type)
  • Circular Flow Multi Hop Cycle Detection strong — A 3-account cycle exists: acct_127 → acct_129 → acct_134 → acct_127, suggesting circular flow and potential money laundering.
  • Duplicate Verification weak — Transactions acct_129_145452 and acct_129_145453 are duplicates, with the same account, direction, amount, date, and narration/reference evidence. (+204 more of this type)
  • Cross Statement Links weak — A debit of 500.00 from acct_129 matches a credit of 500.00 to acct_148 on 2019-01-22, indicating a potential cross-statement link. (+177 more of this type)
  • Failed Reversed Transaction Detection weak — Debit acct_129_146879 is followed by a near-equal credit acct_129_146880 with reversal/reference evidence, indicating a potential failed reversed transaction.
  • Money Flow Graph Construction structural — A money-flow graph with 4044 nodes and 32415 directed transaction edges was constructed, providing insight into the account's transaction patterns.
  • Reversal Clusters weak — Account acct_129 has 3 debit-reversal pairs, indicating systematic transaction reversal activity and potential suspicious behavior.
  • Round Value Debit Patterns weak — Account acct_129 made 17 suspicious round-value debits totaling 378000.00, with repeated amounts, potentially indicating structured transactions.

5 · Money-Flow Graphs

Case Overview Graph

The graph shows 30 clusters with the largest cluster, cluster_001, having 242 accounts, 30114 edges, and a total score of 143984.5804. Cluster_001 also has the most pattern IDs with 18 different patterns. An investigator should take note of cluster_001 due to its large size and high total score, indicating a potentially significant money flow pattern.

Account Interconnection Graph

This static exhibit is filtered for legibility: it shows the material account-to-account relationships and only the most evidential external counterparties, while the full-density network remains available in the UI graph data.

Suspicious Activity Timeline

The graph shows a timeline of suspicious activity from 2009-06-12 to 2026-02-01, covering 202700 transactions. Notably, 490 transactions were flagged and excluded. An investigator should take note of the large number of transactions over this time span, approximately 16 years, to identify potential patterns or anomalies.

Fraud-Pattern Summary

The graph shows various patterns of potentially fraudulent transactions, with the most notable figures including 5617 instances of Duplicate Verification and 1473 instances of Cross Statement Links. An investigator should take note of these large figures, as well as the 818 Shared Upi Identifiers and 433 Structuring Smurfing Detection instances. The data spans a range of patterns, from 1 instance of Money Flow Graph Construction to 5617 instances of Duplicate Verification. The busiest patterns appear to be related to verification and statement links, which an investigator should prioritize for further review.

Score legend

Score RangeWhat It Means
600+Strong evidence across multiple independent pattern types — high investigative priority
300–599Moderate evidence, worth follow-up but less urgent
Below 300Weak or isolated signals, mostly for reference

Generated by the Survey Corps Investigation Engine for CIDECODE · Run validation_original_20260703_215912 · 03 July 2026, 23:23. All findings are investigative leads requiring independent verification.

Data Security & Method (Appendix)

What stays on this machine

  • All fraud detection, scoring and ranking run on this machine (deterministic code).
  • Raw bank statements and extracted transactions never leave the machine.
  • The money-flow graph, clusters and suspicion scores are computed locally.

What is sent to an external service

  • Only small, redacted text samples are sent to the language model to describe a statement's column layout when local parsing is insufficient.
  • For images / scanned PDFs, the page pixels are sent to a vision model (the one unavoidable case).
  • The language model only rewrites explanations into plain language; it never changes a number, account, or decision.

Language-model calls this run: 25 · active
